acts.title: The Gala
acts.body: In the wake of the Siege of Boralus, House Duskmere has spent months funding repairs to Tiragarde. Tonight, they celebrate the work's completion with a gala at their estate.
As Champions of Azeroth, you were invited guests.
Lord Alaric Duskmere is found dead.

acts.title: The Captain Takes Charge
acts.body: Captain Mira Thorne of the Boralus City Watch arrives before the body's even cold, and takes one look at the gathered, panicking guests. 'Nobody leaves. Every single one of you is a suspect until I say otherwise. That includes our Champions of Azeroth.' She turns to you. 'Which is exactly why I need your help. I can't watch this whole gala at once. Move through the grounds, talk to people, find out who actually had reason to want Alaric Duskmere dead. Motive first. We'll worry about how it was done after.'

acts.title: Investigate the Estate
acts.intro: Walk the grounds and talk to everyone: guests, suspects, staff. Most people here have nothing to hide. A few do. Pick up whatever you find and bring it back to the Evidence Table, every piece matters, and Captain Thorne won't move things forward until it's all there.

acts.title: The Suspect Board
acts.intro: Captain Thorne has laid out what you've found on a table by the gate. 'Alright. Drag forward everyone you believe actually had a real motive. Leave off anyone whose story checks out. When you're confident, submit it to me, and know that I'll send you back to think again if you've got it wrong.'

acts.title: Means and Opportunity
acts.body: 'Good work,' Thorne says, looking over your list. 'Now we find out which one of them actually did it, and how.' [PLACEHOLDER: Phase 2 puzzles go here: alibis, timelines, opportunity, the poison itself. This is where the frame-up interrupts you partway through.]

acts.title: The Frame-Up
acts.body: Before you can finish, Commodore Brannigan Voss 'discovers' the murder weapon planted among your belongings. Someone mutters about Tol Dagor, and is immediately shut down: half that prison's still got cannonball holes in it from the last time someone broke a prisoner out. Instead, you're marched down to the docks and thrown in the brig of Duskmere's Providence, her timbers still creaking, not even properly crewed yet.

acts.title: Duskmere's Providence
acts.body: What happens next isn't fully built yet, but here's how the night goes: you pick the brig's lock, slip past a skeleton crew doing their rounds, and find something in the cargo hold that changes everything: manifests showing the poison's rare ingredient moved through this very ship. Old Silas Corwin makes good on his word and shows you a way out through the smuggler's tunnels beneath the harbor. You surface back on the docks. Free, and with enough evidence to make your case.

acts.title: Naming the Culprit
acts.prompt: You corner Lady Isobel Ashgate with everything you've gathered. Cornered, she finally gives up the word scratched into the ship's cargo manifest: the code the smugglers used for their poison shipment. What was it?
acts.hint: [Placeholder: this ties back to the poison-ID puzzle we haven't designed yet.]

acts.title: Case Closed... Or Is It?
acts.body: Lady Ashgate hands over the word, cool as ever, almost too composed for someone just caught out. 'Nightshade,' she says. 'That's what they called it.' But as she says it, her eyes flick, just for a moment, toward the estate, toward the steward, quietly straightening papers by the door, who suddenly finds something very interesting to do with his hands. Ashby was the one panicking about an audit. Ashby had access to every ledger, every shipment record, every excuse to be anywhere in that house at any hour. Maybe Lady Ashgate simply knows how to take a fall gracefully. Or maybe she's not the one who should be taking it at all. Take your answer back to Discord. The case is closed, even if the truth might not be.
